architecture.summary: A neural NER tagger that concatenates multiple pre-trained embeddings as word representations and applies constrained decoding during inference to enforce valid tag transitions. The model is trained with cross-entropy loss (not CRF) but decoding eliminates illegal state transitions post-hoc, combining the representational benefit of multi-embedding concatenation with tag-sequence validity without the computational overhead of a CRF layer.
trained_components: neural encoder with concatenated pre-trained embeddings

biblio.title: Computationally Efficient NER Taggers with Combined Embeddings and Constrained Decoding

claims.statement: 786% speed-up over a contextual embeddings-based tagger without sacrificing strong performance
Multiple pre-trained embeddings concatenated as word representations substantially improve NER performance over a strong baseline with negligible cost
Constraining the tagger during decoding to eliminate illegal transitions improves performance
The concatenation technique works across multiple tasks and datasets
Constrained decoding eliminates the need for a CRF layer at the final network level while maintaining tag coherence
